definition: IG commodity importers initially experience spread tightening on nominal revenue growth before violent repricing when working capital absorption and margin deterioration become visible. This parallels the equity-side OCF/revenue diagnostic where revenue growth masks cash flow deterioration.
evidence: Nominal revenue growth in early commodity inflation stages mechanically improves IG credit metrics before cash flow reality emerges
Parallels the equity-side finding that S&P 500 Industrials showed 15% revenue growth but only 6% OCF growth
open_questions: How long does the false tightening phase typically last before repricing?
Can the OCF/revenue ratio serve as an early warning for IG credit repricing?
